title: arg_session: Ensure created void* types have arch.
body:
Summary:
- The failing trace indicates an exception where a void* placeholder is created without an architecture (arch) context, leading to a ValueError when trying to determine the size of a SimTypePointer.
- Specifically, SimCC.arg_session creates an implicit out-parameter as SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) without propagating the current architecture, which then causes size/lookups to fail later in the calling-convention flow.
- Proposed fix: when creating the implicit void* argument in SimCC.arg_session, attach the current architecture to the pointer type so size can be determined correctly (i.e., SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()).with_arch(self.arch)).
- This keeps arch-propagation consistent across the SimTypePointer and its pointee, preventing the observed crash while preserving existing behavior for other pointer/arch interactions.

Impact:
- Localized change to angr/analyses/calling_convention/calling_convention.py
- No public API changes; behavior becomes more robust for AMD64/SystemV and other arches where implicit void* outparams are used.
- Should reduce or eliminate the ValueError: Can't tell my size without an arch! traceback in scenarios involving implicit void* arguments.

step_plan.summary: Ensure created void* placeholders in SimCC.arg_session carry architecture to prevent size lookups from failing when arch is not propagated. Attach self.arch to the implicit SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) via .with_arch(self.arch).

steps.operation: Modify implicit void* argument creation to be arch-aware
steps.reason: Current implicit out-parameter creation uses SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) without arch, causing size resolution to fail when arch is required. Attaching arch ensures consistent arch propagation and prevents ValueError.
steps.side_effects: Should fix ValueError: Can't tell my size without an arch!; may affect downstream size/lookups and tests; likely requires unit tests to verify arch propagation for implicit void* outparams and ensure no regressions in AMD64/SystemV paths
